message:
feat(model-selector): filter /model overlay to show only credential-configured providers

When /model is invoked without arguments, the selector now opens in a
"configured only" mode that filters the model list to entries where the
user has a valid API key or OAuth token. This prevents presenting a wall
of unconfigured models and surfaces only actionable choices.

Implementation details:
- Add available_models_with_credentials() to PiApp that checks each model
  entry against AuthStorage.resolve_api_key(), filtering out entries with
  no configured credentials
- Add open_model_selector_configured_only() which uses the filtered list
  and sets configured_only scope on the overlay
- Extend ModelSelectorOverlay with source_total and configured_only fields
  to track whether the overlay is in filtered mode and how many total
  models exist (for the "(N/M)" counter display)
- Update the /model help text from "Show or change the current model" to
  "Open model selector or switch directly"
- Add visual indicators in the model selector UI: a subtitle explaining
  the filtered view and suppressing the "(type to filter)" placeholder
  when in configured-only mode
- Update tests: the no-args /model test now expects the configured-only
  selector behavior, with a new test verifying that only models with API
  keys appear and the "(1/2)" total counter is correct
- Cargo.lock and submodule pointer updated from concurrent work

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
